pages.raw_text:
special Correspondence to The Press


BOISE, July 12.IThe strongest
figure in the courtroom today Is
Mrs. Haywood, the invalid wife of
the man now on the witness stand
in defense of his own life. With
her 2 little daughters by her side
She follows every word and action
with intense interest, and her eyes
never waver from her husband.


If Haywood is uttering a single
untruth he is deceiving the woman.
who worships him and to whom he
has given the tenderest care in her
years of affiiction. The constant
expression Of her face shows that
she instantly accepts every state
ment he makes without a trace of
question or suspicion.


Is 1t possible that man full OF
such tenderness to the poor little
invalid wife, and who had won
such ,affection from his children,
was capable Of the calculating.
cold Hooded assassination charged
against him?
